2604.09101.title: CLIP-Inspector: Model-Level Backdoor Detection for Prompt-Tuned CLIP via OOD Trigger Inversion
2604.09101.paper_summary: Organisations with limited data and computational resources increasingly outsource model training to Machine Learning as a Service (MLaaS) providers, who adapt vision-language models (VLMs) such as CLIP to downstream tasks via prompt tuning rather than training from scratch. This semi-honest setting creates a security risk where a malicious provider can follow the prompt-tuning protocol yet implant a backdoor, forcing triggered inputs to be classified into an attacker-chosen class, even for out-of-distribution (OOD) data. Such backdoors leave encoders untouched, making them undetectable to existing methods that focus on encoder corruption. Other data-level methods that sanitize data before training or during inference, also fail to answer the critical question, "Is the delivered model backdoored or not?" To address this model-level verification problem, we introduce CLIP-Inspector (CI), a backdoor detection method designed for prompt-tuned CLIP models. Assuming white-box access to the delivered model and a pool of unlabeled OOD images, CI reconstructs possible triggers for each class to determine if the model exhibits backdoor behaviour or not. Additionally, we demonstrate that using CI's reconstructed trigger for fine-tuning on correctly labeled triggered inputs enables us to re-align the model and reduce backdoor effectiveness. Through extensive experiments across ten datasets and four backdoor attacks, we demonstrate that CI can reconstruct effective triggers in a single epoch using only 1,000 OOD images, achieving a 94% detection accuracy (47/50 models). Compared to adapted trigger-inversion baselines, CI yields a markedly higher AUROC score (0.973 vs 0.495/0.687), thus enabling the vetting and post-hoc repair of prompt-tuned CLIP models to ensure safe deployment.
2604.09101.paper_summary_zh: 組織在數據和計算資源有限的情況下，越來越多地將模型訓練外包給機器學習即服務（MLaaS）提供商，這些提供商通過提示調整將視覺語言模型（VLMs）如 CLIP 適應於下游任務，而不是從頭開始訓練。這種半誠實的環境創造了一種安全風險，惡意提供商可以遵循提示調整協議，但植入後門，迫使觸發的輸入被分類為攻擊者選擇的類別，即使是對於分佈外（OOD）數據。這種後門使編碼器保持不變，從而使其對於專注於編碼器損壞的現有方法來說無法檢測。其他在訓練前或推理期間清理數據的數據級方法，也無法回答關鍵問題：“交付的模型是否存在後門？” 為了解決這一模型級驗證問題，我們引入了 CLIP-Inspector（CI），這是一種專為提示調整的 CLIP 模型設計的後門檢測方法。假設對交付模型具有白盒訪問權限以及一組未標記的 OOD 圖像，CI 重建每個類的可能觸發器，以確定模型是否表現出後門行為。此外，我們展示了使用 CI 重建的觸發器對正確標記的觸發輸入進行微調，使我們能夠重新對齊模型並減少後門的有效性。通過在十個數據集和四種後門攻擊中進行廣泛實驗，我們證明 CI 只需使用 1,000 張 OOD 圖像就能在單個時期內重建有效的觸發器，達到 94% 的檢測準確率（47/50 模型）。與適應的觸發反轉基線相比，CI 產生了顯著更高的 AUROC 分數（0.973 對 0.495/0.687），從而使得對提示調整的 CLIP 模型進行審核和事後修復以確保安全部署成為可能。
2604.09101.author: Akshit Jindal et.al.
2604.09101.authors: Akshit Jindal, Saket Anand, Chetan Arora, Vikram Goyal
